fields.ECO.CI.CI.AppliesToProducts: C&E/Azure/Azure Cloud/Azure/Azure Cloud Services|||C&E/Azure/Azure Cloud/Azure/Azure Active Directory|||ASG/Office 365 Multi Tenant/Exchange Cloud/Exchange Online/Exchange Online|||ASG/Office 365 Multi Tenant/Identity Cloud/O365 Identity/Office 365 Identity Management
fields.System.Description:
Need additional information added to the KB article for this scenario. Note needs to be added indicating there are several side-effects of changing the UPN prefix. Suggested change:

Scenario 2: The user has a license

To update the UPN of a user who was assigned a license, follow these steps:

  1. Start the Azure Active Directory Module for Windows PowerShell, and then connect to Azure Active Directory (Azure AD). For more information about how to do this, go to the following Microsoft website:
    Connect to Azure AD Using Windows PowerShell
    (http://aka.ms/aadposh#BKMK_connect)
  2. Run the following Windows PowerShell cmdlet:

    Set-MsolUserPrincipalName -UserPrincipalName [CurrentUPN] -NewUserPrincipalName [NewUPN]


Note: Be aware that modifying the UPN prefix in Azure AD may result in the following Exchange Online attributes being modified:


UserPrincipalName

WindowsLiveID

MicrosoftOnlineServicesID

Alias

Smtp email address

Sip address

fields.System.Description:
There have been a UI change in the diagnostic requiring a small change to the text in this article.  Also there's a need to modify the section titled "How to run Directory Synchronization Diagnostic".  The second URL is currently wrong. 


BEFORE


If a customer hasn't recently run the diagnostic, Support may ask the customer to browse to http://aka.ms/hrcsync from their directory synchronization server.

On the server, open Internet Explorer, and then browse to http://aka.ms/dsup .


AFTER - DESIRED


If a customer hasn't recently run the diagnostic, Support may ask the customer to browse to http://aka.ms/hrcsync from their directory synchronization server.

On the server, open Internet Explorer, and then browse to http://aka.ms/hrcsync .

fields.ECO.CI.CI.SupportTopics: Routing Azure Active Directory/Active Directory Synchronization/Password does not synchronize

ECO.CI.CI.TechReviewers.displayName: Bill Fiddes; Rob Greene; Andreas Kjellman; arrenc

fields.System.Description:
Customers are struggling to get passwords synchronized from prem to Cloud using DIRSYNC, AADSYNC and AAD Connect


Support consistently works 100 - 115 calls per month.


Possible Root causes include


  1. "Synchronize now" checkbox was unchecked during AADSYNC | AAD Connect install

  2. AADSYNC and AAD Connect Only\Account not subject to OU filtering fails to sync password when subject to OU filtering

  3. Account subject to attribute filtering fails to sync password after becoming subject to attribute filtering

  4. Passwords don't sync until 1st full sync has completed.

  5. Renaming computername of AADConnect | AADSYNC computer causes sync failures

  6. Other reasons not currently in this list. For example, anything that prevents objects from syncing globally or on specific objects would prevent child attributes from synchronizing.
fields.System.Title: AAD: Create public facing content on reasons why Sync Clients fail sync Passwords from prem to AAD

attributes.name: RE Bemis-PREVIEW KB 2855271 - How to troubleshoot password synchronization when using an Azure AD sync appliance.msg
